title: Ralph Phase M: optimization-ceiling documentation + 10-run variance characterization
context: Prior two Ralph cycles (K1: harness + FP32_ACC=1 default; L: bfp8+split dead-ends) established that the remaining speed+accuracy headroom in the Python/host/op-config layer is at the 0.3-0.5 t/s noise floor. Further kernel-level wins require tt-metal kernel forks (30+ min rebuild cost per attempt) which are high-risk for a time-bounded Ralph session. This Phase M cycle characterizes the noise floor rigorously so future kernel-fork sessions have a calibrated baseline, and documents the optimization ceiling as a reference for Ralph sessions that follow.

stories.title: 5-run bench variance characterization on current HEAD
stories.finding: 5 back-to-back bench_batch32 runs on 75a65b5: 73.82, 74.14, 74.30, 73.99, 73.97 t/s. Mean 74.04, stddev 0.17, min-max spread 0.48. Confirms ~0.5 t/s noise band claim. Kernel wins must clear > 0.5 t/s mean-over-3-runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Noise band confirmed at ~0.5 t/s — DONE

stories.title: Commit docs/session_14_optimization_ceiling.md with Phase K+L+M findings
stories.finding: docs/session_14_optimization_ceiling.md created (under 200 lines) with baseline, noise floor from M1 (74.04 mean, 0.17 stddev), PCC figures, tested-rejected lever list (bfp8 KV / ttnn.split / SDPA K2-K4 redirect / attn_sub_norm amp falsification), real drift engine identification (Q/K/V ternary matmul chain), and a decision tree for the next kernel-fork session. Referenced from docs/STATUS.md:146 in the Design+decision docs table.
acceptanceCriteria: All required content (baseline/noise/PCC/rejected levers/decision tree) — DONE
